is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of severe malaria caused by Plasmodium falciparum in Gambian children; cases defined by asexual forms of P. falciparum on blood film.

reports_target_markers.evidence: This is a human GWA study of host genetic resistance loci (HbS/HBB, ABO, G6PD, etc.). No P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, Pfcytb) are genotyped or reported.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Reports human SNP allele frequencies and odds ratios, not parasite drug-resistance codon frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples from the Kombos region of The Gambia (sub-national), Royal Victoria Hospital, Banjul.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No parasite marker data and no clearly bounded collection window for drug-resistance frequencies is relevant; the study is a host-genetics GWA study.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates collected from Gambian children admitted to hospital in Banjul.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Part of MalariaGEN multicenter consortium: 'This study forms part of a multicenter investigation of genetic resistance to malaria' and 'issues are currently being addressed by the MalariaGEN consortium in a multicenter study across 11 different malaria-endemic populations.'

supplement.evidence: Even with supplementary tables, this paper concerns human host genetics, not P. falciparum drug-resistance markers, so no relevant extractable parasite data would be present.

assessment.summary: This is a genome-wide association study of severe malaria examining HUMAN host genetic resistance loci (HbS/HBB, ABO, G6PD, and others) in Gambian children. It does not genotype or report any P. falciparum drug-resistance markers or codon frequencies. It is therefore out of scope for a parasite drug-resistance marker database.
exclusion_reasons: No P. falciparum drug-resistance target markers reported (this is a human host-genetics GWA study).
No extractable parasite per-locus drug-resistance frequencies.
